notes: Primary key. Scale is 0 so lossless cast to INT64 is safe. Post-load uniqueness validation required per acceptance criteria.
BigQuery STRING is unbounded; max 50 characters from source poses no truncation risk.
BigQuery STRING is unbounded; max 50 characters from source poses no truncation risk.
BigQuery STRING is unbounded; max 100 characters from source poses no truncation risk. Pre-load null validation applies as column is NOT NULL.
Nullable in source; NULL values pass through unchanged. Pre-load validation must confirm null handling strategy is applied consistently.
Direct DATE-to-DATE mapping. MySQL date format (YYYY-MM-DD) aligns natively with BigQuery DATE; no format conversion required.
Tertiary clustering column. Moderate cardinality job codes improve pruning for job-role analytical queries.
BigQuery NUMERIC supports up to 29 digits with 9 decimal places; decimal(8,2) fits with no precision loss.
Nullable; decimal(4,2) fits comfortably within BigQuery NUMERIC bounds. NULL values preserved. Pre-load null report must capture rows where this is NULL.
Self-referential FK to employee_id. Scale is 0, lossless cast to INT64. NULL indicates top-level or unmanaged employee; NULL constraint violation logging must exclude this column.
Leading clustering column. Scale is 0, lossless cast to INT64. Nullable; NULL values land in the leading cluster slot without error. High-value for department-scoped analytics pruning.
Secondary clustering column. Low-cardinality values (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE) maximise cluster pruning efficiency when combined with department_id.

Partition column with DAY granularity. NOT NULL guarantees no rows fall into the __NULL__ partition. Scopes both full historical load windows and incremental load boundaries. MySQL TIMESTAMP maps to BigQuery TIMESTAMP (UTC); timezone alignment must be validated pre-load.

Delta tracking column for incremental loads. Nullable; incremental pipeline WHERE clause must use COALESCE(UPDATED_DATE, CREATED_DATE) >= :last_watermark to safely capture records never updated. MySQL TIMESTAMP maps to BigQuery TIMESTAMP (UTC); timezone alignment must be validated pre-load.
